articles.title: A very special traffic cone arrives in Boston, courtesy of our new friends in Glasgow
articles.description:
The Tartan Army is the gift that keeps on giving.

The post A very special traffic cone arrives in Boston, courtesy of our new friends in Glasgow appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Glasgow's Tartan Army gifted a unique traffic cone to Boston.
The cone symbolizes friendship between Boston and Glasgow, enhancing cultural ties.
Residents can view the cone, promoting local tourism and community engagement.

articles.title: Bipartisan group of senators introduces legislation to avert looming Social Security shortfall
articles.description:
“The longer Congress waits, the more difficult it will be to address the program’s financial shortfall,” Sen. Dick Durbin, D-Ill., one of the bill’s authors, said in a statement.

The post Bipartisan group of senators introduces legislation to avert looming Social Security shortfall appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bipartisan senators introduce legislation to address Social Security shortfall.
Sen. Dick Durbin emphasizes urgency to prevent worsening financial issues.
Potential changes could directly affect Boston residents relying on Social Security benefits.

articles.title: 3 more MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud, falsifying track inspections
articles.description:
Federal prosecutors also allege several now-former employees at the MBTA’s Cabot Yard worked on personal vehicles while on duty.

The post 3 more MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud, falsifying track inspections appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Three MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud and falsifying track inspections.
Allegations include working on personal vehicles while on duty at Cabot Yard.
Increased scrutiny on MBTA operations may affect service reliability for Boston commuters.

articles.title: Trump administration orders ICE to suspend most vehicle stops after two deadly shootings
articles.description:
The order came after an ICE officer shot and killed a Colombian driver Monday in Maine and a week after one shot and killed a motorist in Houston.

The post Trump administration orders ICE to suspend most vehicle stops after two deadly shootings appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: ICE suspends most vehicle stops following two fatal shootings involving officers.
Colombian driver shot by ICE officer in Maine on July 10, 2023.
Boston residents may see reduced ICE presence and fewer vehicle stops.
